domain_hint: Conjugate heat transfer model for multi-pass serpentine turbine blade cooling channel, ANSYS Fluent, high-pressure air coolant, flight-critical design support
ambiguity_notes: (1) Turbulence model inconsistency: k-ω SST used in code verification benchmark (Slide 6) but realizable k-ε used in all production runs, with no documented justification — extractor must reconcile which model is actually in use and whether verification covers the production configuration. (2) Radiation neglect contradiction: the main model documentation (Slide 3) states radiation is justified as negligible, but internal memo TN-2024-017 (also cited) quantifies a 4–7% radiation contribution at rib-tip temperatures above 1100 K — the two sources directly conflict and neither is resolved in the current revision. (3) Intended use scope creep: Slide 2 states the model was originally scoped for "preliminary design guidance only" but is now being used to set inspection intervals for a flight-critical component, with no formal re-scoping — the consequence level claimed in the current document contradicts the model's original qualification basis. (4) TC-31 outlier handling: Slide 7 reports a 38 K maximum prediction error attributed to "possible thermocouple installation error" with no re-test or corrective action, while Slide 8 simultaneously claims turbulence model uncertainty is "expected to be secondary" — these two statements are in tension because the outlier location (bend exit) is precisely where RANS secondary-flow errors are largest, suggesting the error attribution may be incorrect.
